back_translation: Then Elihu continued:
'Hear my words, O wise men; listen to me, O men of knowledge.
For the ear tests words, as the mouth tastes food.
Let us choose for ourselves what is right; let us learn together what is good.
For Job has said, 'I am righteous, but God has deprived me of justice.'
'Would I lie about my case? My wound is incurable, though I am without transgression.'
What man is like Job, who drinks up derision like water?
He keeps company with evildoers and walks with wicked men.
For he has said, 'A man gains nothing by delighting in God.'
Therefore, O men of understanding, listen to me. Far be it from God to do evil, and from the Almighty to act unjustly.
For He repays a man according to his deeds and brings upon him the consequences of his ways.
Truly, God does not do wickedly, and the Almighty does not pervert justice.
Who gave Him charge over the earth? Who set Him over the whole world?
If He were to set His heart on gathering back His Spirit and breath to Himself,
all flesh would perish together, and mankind would return to the dust.
If you have understanding, hear this; listen to the sound of my words.
Can one who hates justice govern? Will you condemn the One who is righteous and mighty,
who says to a king, 'You are worthless,' and to nobles, 'You are wicked,'
who is not partial to princes and does not favor the rich over the poor? For all are the work of His hands.
They die in an instant, in the middle of the night; people convulse and pass away, and the mighty are removed without a human hand.
For His eyes watch the ways of man, and He sees his every step.
There is no darkness or deep shadow where the workers of iniquity can hide.
For God need not examine a man further, or bring him in for judgment.
He shatters the mighty without inquiry and sets up others in their place.
Therefore He knows their deeds; He overthrows them in the night, and they are crushed.
He punishes them for their wickedness in public view,
because they turned aside from Him and had no regard for any of His ways.
They caused the cry of the poor to come up to Him, and He heard the cry of the afflicted.
But when He remains silent, who can condemn Him? And when He hides His face, who can see Him? This is so for both nation and individual alike.
so that godless men may not reign or lay snares for the people.
Suppose someone says to God, 'I have borne my punishment; I will not offend again.
Teach me what I cannot see; if I have done wrong, I will not do it again.'
Should God repay you on your terms when you have rejected Him? You must choose, not I; so tell me what you know.
Men of understanding will tell me, and the wise men who hear me will say:
'Job speaks without knowledge, and his words lack insight.'
Let Job be tested to the utmost, because he answers like a wicked man.
For he adds rebellion to his sin; he claps his hands in mockery among us and multiplies his words against God.'
